title: Chapter 5 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of Flutter widgets!

questions.question: What is the root widget of a Flutter app typically?
questions.explanation: MaterialApp is commonly the root, providing navigation and theming.

questions.question: A StatelessWidget can change its internal state.
questions.explanation: StatelessWidget is immutable; use StatefulWidget for mutable state.

questions.question: To update the UI in a StatefulWidget, you call _____.
questions.explanation: setState() triggers a rebuild.

questions.question: Which widget provides a basic material design layout structure?
questions.explanation: Scaffold implements the basic material design visual layout.

questions.question: An IconButton is a type of button that displays an icon.
questions.explanation: Yes, IconButton is a clickable icon.

questions.question: How do you disable a button?
questions.explanation: When onPressed is null, the button is disabled.

questions.question: The widget that arranges children vertically is called _____.
questions.explanation: Column arranges children in a vertical direction.

questions.question: You can add an image from the internet using Image.network.
questions.explanation: Image.network loads an image from a URL.

questions.question: Which parameter of Container adds space inside the container?
questions.explanation: Padding adds space inside the container around its child.

questions.question: The top bar in a Scaffold is called _____.
questions.explanation: AppBar is the typical top bar.

questions.question: A const constructor allows Flutter to reuse widget instances.
questions.explanation: Const widgets are canonicalized and can improve performance.

questions.question: Which widget would you use to display a simple string of text?
questions.explanation: Text widget displays a string.

questions.question: To add a floating action button, use the _____ parameter of Scaffold.
questions.explanation: The floatingActionButton parameter accepts a widget, typically a FloatingActionButton.

questions.question: A Row widget arranges its children horizontally.
questions.explanation: Row lays out children in a horizontal array.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the build method in a widget?
questions.explanation: build returns the widget tree for this widget.

questions.question: The widget that displays a Material Design raised button is _____.
questions.explanation: ElevatedButton is the current recommended raised button.

questions.question: You can use CircleAvatar to display a profile image.
questions.explanation: CircleAvatar is commonly used for profile pictures with a circular clip.

questions.question: Which property of Text changes the font size?
questions.explanation: style: TextStyle(fontSize: ...) sets the font size.

questions.question: To load a local image, use Image._____.
questions.explanation: Image.asset loads an image from the app's assets.

questions.question: The key parameter is used to preserve state when widgets move in the tree.
questions.explanation: Keys help Flutter identify widgets and preserve state during tree changes.

questions.question: What does the SizedBox widget do when given a height and width?
options: It adds space around its child
It forces its child to have that size
It creates an empty box of that size
questions.explanation: SizedBox creates a box of specified dimensions; if it has a child, it sizes the child to that size.

questions.question: The widget that can have a background color, border, and padding is _____.
questions.explanation: Container combines painting, positioning, and sizing.

questions.question: A StatelessWidget can have mutable fields.
questions.explanation: All fields in a StatelessWidget should be final; otherwise, use StatefulWidget.

questions.question: Which widget is used to show a circular progress indicator?
questions.explanation: CircularProgressIndicator shows a spinning progress indicator.

questions.question: To handle a button press, you provide a callback to the _____ parameter.
questions.explanation: Buttons use onPressed for click handling.
